name: Ascent to Torcal de Antequera from Antequera – circular through the white villages
name_original: Subida al Torcal de Antequera desde Antequera – circular por los pueblos blancos
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Antequera traverse a diverse landscape characterized by dramatic karstic limestone formations, fertile agricultural valleys, and challenging mountain passes. The region, nestled in the heart of Andalusia, offers varied terrain for road cyclists, from demanding ascents to more moderate rides. These routes often lead through picturesque white villages and past scenic reservoirs. Best road cycling routes around Antequera The most popular road cycling route is Ascent to Torcal de Antequera from Antequera – circular through the white villages, a 50.7 miles (81.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route features a significant climb to the unique El Torcal natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of the Casasola Reservoir – Almogía Cycling Viewpoint loop from Antequera AV, a difficult 55.6 miles (89.4 km) path. This route offers expansive views of the Casasola Reservoir and winds through agricultural valleys. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto El Saltillo (885 m) – Torcal de Antequera loop from Antequera AV, a 22.3 miles (35.9 km) trail leading through limestone formations and mountain passes, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Road cycling around Antequera is defined by karstic limestone formations, agricultural valleys, and challenging mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from demanding ascents to more moderate rides. The routes in Antequera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Antequera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Antequera?
A: Road cycling routes around Antequera offer a diverse landscape. You'll encounter dramatic karstic limestone formations, particularly around El Torcal, fertile agricultural valleys with lemon and orange groves, and challenging mountain passes. The region's varied terrain provides options from demanding ascents to more moderate rides through picturesque white villages and past scenic reservoirs.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginner road cyclists in Antequera?
A: While many routes in Antequera feature significant elevation gains and are rated as difficult, there are also options for less experienced riders. The Guadalhorce Valley, for instance, offers less steep, paved roads ideal for a more moderate ride. Look for routes with lower elevation gain if you're just starting out.

Q: What are some of the most scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer breathtaking views. The climb to El Torcal de Antequera rewards cyclists with panoramic vistas of the surrounding hills and the impressive rock massif. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40640820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Casasola Reservoir – Fuente de Almogía loop from Antequera AV&lt;/a&gt; provide expansive views of the Casasola Reservoir. You can also find routes that offer glimpses of the dramatic Desfiladero de los Gaitanes gorge.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Antequera?
A: Antequera is a great destination for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather conditions, with comfortable temperatures for longer rides. Summers can be very hot, especially in the valleys, so early morning rides are recommended if cycling during this season. Winters are mild, but you might encounter cooler temperatures at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Antequera are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942687474/ascent-to-torcal-de-antequera-from-antequera-circular-through-the-white-villages" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Torcal de Antequera from Antequera – circular through the white villages&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions to see near the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural wonders. You can cycle towards the extraordinary karstic landscape of El Torcal de Antequera, a UNESCO World Heritage site. The area is also close to the dramatic Desfiladero de los Gaitanes gorge, home to the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caminito del Rey&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morrón de Mal Infierno&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5236339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de los Gaitanes Gorge&lt;/a&gt; itself.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, Antequera is steeped in history. While cycling, you might pass by traditional whitewashed mountain villages like Álora, Cártama, and Pizarra. The region is also home to the Antequera Dolmens Site, a UNESCO World Heritage site showcasing prehistoric megalithic tombs. You could also find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4203187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mozarabic Rock-Cut Church of Bobastro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long are the road cycling routes around Antequera typically?
A: The routes vary significantly in length. You can find shorter loops, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14594729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto El Saltillo (885 m) – Torcal de Antequera loop from Antequera AV&lt;/a&gt; at about 35.9 miles (57.8 km), or longer, more challenging rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40640820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Casasola Reservoir – Fuente de Almogía loop from Antequera AV&lt;/a&gt; which is around 55.5 miles (89.4 km).

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Antequera?
A: The road cycling routes around Antequera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs to unique geological formations like El Torcal, and the scenic rides through agricultural valleys and past reservoirs.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly road cycling in the area?
A: While many routes feature significant climbs, the Guadalhorce Valley offers flatter, paved roads that are more suitable for families or those seeking a less strenuous ride. These routes often pass through agricultural landscapes and traditional villages, providing a pleasant experience for all skill levels.

Q: What are the parking facilities like for road cyclists in Antequera?
A: Antequera, as a starting point for many routes, generally offers various parking options within the city. For routes heading towards El Torcal, there is a visitor center at the top with dedicated parking facilities. It's advisable to check specific starting points for available parking before your ride.

Q: Can I use public transport with my bike to access routes around Antequera?
A: While Antequera is well-connected by public transport, bringing bikes on buses or trains can sometimes be restricted or require special arrangements, especially for full-sized road bikes. It's best to check with local transport providers directly regarding their policies for carrying bicycles.
tips: Tough climb but the views are worth it. The first half out of Villanueva de la Concepción is the toughest, especially if you already stopped for a coffee and sat down for too long!

waypoints.name: Climb from Villanueva de la Concepción to El Torcal de Antequera
